title: asteriscus aquaticus subsp. nanus
intro: Asteriscus aquaticus subsp. nanus, commonly known as Dwarf Marsh Cudweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Asteriscus aquaticus subsp. nanus include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and ease digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
